message.content:
Explore the dns-de-rte-orchestrator project at /Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/ to understand the current SAM/Lambda setup.

Specifically:
1. Read the full template.yaml and report its current structure — existing resources, parameters, the CreateFunction Lambda definition (especially its handler, env vars, role, VPC).
2. Read samconfig.toml and report relevant deployment config.
3. Read the lambda handler file referenced by CreateFunction (dns_de_rte_orchestrator.creator_lambda.lambda_handler) — what does it expect as input? Does it already handle API Gateway events (APIGatewayProxyEvent), or only direct invocation/other event types?
4. Find and summarize the Makefile used for SAM build.
5. Check if there's any existing API Gateway configuration or HTTP handling code.
6. Check README.md for context on how this service is intended to be invoked.

Report back:
- Full CreateFunction definition and all related resources/roles
- Whether the lambda handler is API-Gateway-ready or needs adaptation
- What IAM/roles pattern is used (ServerlessAccessManager)
- Any existing REST-related code or utilities
- File paths + line numbers for critical items

Under 600 words. Read-only exploration only.

content.text: I'll explore the project structure in parallel.

content.content:
1	AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
2	Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
3	Description: >
4	  Right to Erasure Orchestrator.
5	  Originally a replacement for the Tines Orchestrator, this stack primarily
6	  consists of 2 AWS Lambdas that communicate with DynamoDB
7	
8	Parameters:
9	  Environment:
10	    Description: Environment
11	    Type: String
12	  DynamoDBTable:
13	    Description: The DynamoDB table to create new RTE requests in
14	    Type: String
15	  DynamoDBTableGSI:
16	    Description: The DynamoDB table GSI to query for RTE requests
17	    Type: String
18	
19	Resources:
20	  ServerlessAccessManager:
21	    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
22	    Properties:
23	      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
24	      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-create-request-${Environment}'
25	      VpcExecution: "true"
26	      DatadogLogging: "true"
27	      Targets:
28	        DynamoDBTable:
29	          Type: DynamoDB
30	          Properties:
31	            Table: !Ref DynamoDBTable
32	
33	  CreateFunction:
34	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
35	    Metadata:
36	      BuildMethod: makefile
37	    Properties:
38	      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
39	      CodeUri: ./
40	      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.creator_lambda.lambda_handler
41	      Runtime: python3.13
42	      Tracing: Active
43	      Description: Create RTE requests
44	      MemorySize: 256
45	      Timeout: 30
46	      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 10
47	      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
48	      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
49	      Environment:
50	        Variables:
51	          REPOSITORY_TYPE: "dynamodb"
52	          DYNAMO_DB_TABLE: !Ref DynamoDBTable
53	          DYNAMO_DB_INDEX: !Ref DynamoDBTableGSI
54	          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "create-erasure-request-${Environment}"
55

1	version = 0.1
2	
3	[default.build.parameters]
4	beta_features = true
5	
6	[default.sync.parameters]
7	beta_features = true
8	
9	[dev.deploy.parameters]
10	region = "eu-west-1"
11	role_arn = "arn:aws:iam::334512769709:role/CFDeploy"
12	s3_bucket = "hbi-dns-infra-datalake"
13	s3_prefix = "data/rte-orchestrator/dev"
14	tags = [
15	    "application=rte-orchestrator",
16	    "name=rte-orchestrator",
17	    "vertical=data",
18	    "owner=data",
19	    "team=de",
20	    "managed_by=aws-sam",
21	    "product=right-to-erasure",
22	    "project=right-to-erasure",
23	    "repository=https://gitlab.com/hnbi/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator"
24	]
25	capabilities = "CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND"
26	stack_name = "data-rte-orchestrator-dev"
27	parameter_overrides = """
28	  Environment=dev
29	  DynamoDBTable=rte-orchestrator-dev
30	  DynamoDBTableGSI=idx-by-state-dev
31	"""
32	
33	[prod.deploy.parameters]
34	region = "eu-west-1"
35	role_arn = "arn:aws:iam::334512769709:role/CFDeploy"
36	s3_bucket = "hbi-dns-infra-datalake"
37	s3_prefix = "data/rte-orchestrator/prod"
38	tags = [
39	    "application=rte-orchestrator",
40	    "name=rte-orchestrator",
41	    "vertical=data",
42	    "owner=data",
43	    "team=de",
44	    "managed_by=aws-sam",
45	    "product=right-to-erasure",
46	    "project=right-to-erasure",
47	    "repository=https://gitlab.com/hnbi/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator"
48	]
49	capabilities = "CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND"
50	stack_name = "data-rte-orchestrator-prod"
51	parameter_overrides = """
52	  Environment=prod
53	  DynamoDBTable=rte-orchestrator-prod
54	  DynamoDBTableGSI=idx-by-state-prod
55	"""
56

1	# This is a Makefile for building packages to deploy to AWS with AWS SAM
2	.DEFAULT_GOAL := help
3	
4	.PHONY: help clean build-CreateFunction
5	
6	help: ## Show help
7		@echo "Usage: make [target]"
8		@echo "This should be run by AWS SAM"
9	
10	build-CreateFunction:
11		uv export --no-dev --format requirements-txt -o .aws-sam-requirements.txt
12		uv pip install --python 3.13 --target "${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/" -r .aws-sam-requirements.txt
13		cp -r src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator "${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/"
14		rm -f .aws-sam-requirements.txt
15	
16	clean:
17		rm -f .aws-sam-requirements.txt
18

1	# GDPR Right to Erasure Orchestration Platform
2	
3	[![made-with-python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Made%20with-Python-1f425f.svg)](https://www.python.org/)
4	[![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black)
5	[![pipeline status](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/badges/master/pipeline.svg)](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/commits/master)
6	![coverage](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/badges/master/coverage.svg?job=test)
7	
8	This project was originally created to replace the
9	[Tines](https://www.tines.com/) based Right to Erasure Orchestrator.
10	
11	## Project Goals
12	
13	The primary goal of this project is to replace the existing Tines based
14	orchestrator for subject erasure requests in regards to the GDPR legislation.
15	
16	The main problems being solved are as followed:
17	
18	- The costs related to the use of Tines
19	- The use of Jira as a point of integration and state storage
20	
21	This project also moves the ownership of the orchestration into the Data
22	Engineering team.
23	
24	## Project Details
25	
26	This project is built using Python 3.13+ and uses
27	[uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) as a package manager and build tool.
28	
29	## Development
30	
31	After cloning this repository you should run:
32	
33	```sh
34	uv sync --all-groups
35	uv run prek install
36	```
37	
38	This should create a virtual environment under `.venv` in the local directory,
39	download all dependencies, and install a Git pre-commit hook.
40	
41	Generally speaking, if you want to run a normal Python command you should
42	prefix it with `uv run`.
43	
44	### Testing
45	
46	This project uses `pytest` for unit testing. These should be run using the
47	following command:
48	
49	```sh
50	uv run pytest
51	```
52	
53	`pytest-cov` is used as an extension that can help you see how much coverage
54	the tests are actually giving. You can run this as follows:
55	
56	```sh
57	uv run pytest --cov=dns_de_rte_orchestrator
58	```
59	
60	There isn't a set test coverage percentage code should have, but key files
61	should generally have more coverage than others.
62	
63	### Structure
64	
65	The key entry points for AWS Lambda are:
66	
67	- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/processor_lambda.py`
68	- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py`
69	
70	The storage logic sits primarily in the `RequestRepository` parent class and its
71	implementations in `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_repository.py`.
72	
73	The business logic sits in the `RequestProcessor` class in
74	`src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py`.
75	
76	### Managing Dependencies
77	
78	`pip` commands should not be used to manage dependencies, instead you should
79	use commands such as:
80	
81	```sh
82	# To add a library dependency
83	uv add library
84	# To add a development dependency only
85	uv add --dev pytest
86	# To remove a dependency
87	uv remove library
88	```
89	
90	This should update the `uv.lock` file, but if in doubt you can run `uv lock`
91	to regenerate it.
92	
93	You can attempt to upgrade packages using the following `uv` command:
94	
95	```sh
96	uv lock --upgrade-package library
97	```
98	
99	## Deployment
100	
101	The code in this project is deployed as 2 separate AWS Lambdas using [AWS
102	SAM](https://aws.amazon.com/serverless/sam/).
103	
104	The versions of Python used during development, testing, and deployment should
105	all match as closely as possible.
106	
107	GitLab runners are used to execute tests and deployments. The configuration for
108	this is managed in `.gitlab-ci.yml`.
109	
110	### SAM
111	
112	AWS SAM uses a custom `Makefile` for building the package that gets uploaded to
113	AWS. This `Makefile` should not be invoked manually. The gist of it is:
114	
115	- It generates a `requirements.txt` with all the non-dev dependencies based on
116	  the `uv.lock` file
117	- It installs all these requirements into the artifact directory
118	- It copies the project `src/` code into the artifact directory
119	- It deletes the `requirements.txt` file
120	
121	The reason for the custom process is to reduce the size of the uploaded
122	artifact and to keep it replicable across different AWS SAM versions.
123	
124	> [!note]
125	> `sam local invoke` has problems when combined with the
126	> [ServerlessAccessManager](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/platform-as-a-service/serverless-applications/serverless-access-manager/)
127	> that the DSO team provide for us to use. You'll need to comment it out and
128	> any lines that reference it in order to get `sam local invoke` to work.
129	
130	To do a manual deployment you'll need to do the following:
131	
132	```sh
133	# First run the build process, this will execute the Makefile
134	sam build
135	# Next deploy
136	sam deploy \
137	  --config-file ../../samconfig.toml \
138	  --config-env dev \
139	  --template-file .aws-sam/build/template.yaml
140	```
141	
142	Note that you'll need to point at the `samconfig.toml` in the project root. By
143	default AWS SAM will try and search the same directory as the template file for
144	the `samconfig.toml`.
145	
146	## Code Quality Tools
147	
148	This project makes use of several tools to ensure consistent code quality.
149	
150	You can run these tools locally alongside `pytest` by running:
151	
152	```sh
153	./local-ci.sh
154	```
155	
156	### prek
157	
158	[prek](https://prek.j178.dev/) is a replacement for the
159	[pre-commit](https://pre-commit.com/) framework. Both allow you to run scripts
160	and checks before a Git commit is created.
161	
162	`prek` is primarily used to run the other code quality tools and ensure that
163	the repository remains in a high quality state.
164	
165	You will need to install the Git pre-commit hooks once after cloning the
166	repository by running:
167	
168	```sh
169	uv run prek install
170	```
171	
172	This should force `prek` to run before all local commits.
173	
174	You can also manually run `prek` by using:
175	
176	```sh
177	uv run prek
178	```
179	
180	The `prek` configuration is stored in `prek.toml`. It configures many
181	pre-commit checks and runs `ruff`, `basedpyright` and `black`.
182	
183	### ruff
184	
185	[ruff](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/) is both a fast linter AND code formatter
186	for Python. This project primarily uses it to lint code.
187	
188	You can run it via:
189	
190	```sh
191	uv run ruff check
192	```
193	
194	This can prevent common Python mistakes.
195	
196	### basedpyright
197	
198	[basedpyright](https://docs.basedpyright.com/latest/) is a fork of `pyright`.
199	It works as a static type-checker for Python code.
200	
201	You should include type hints on as much code as possible to help it out and
202	manage programmer expectations. You should also avoid using deprecated types
203	along with the `Any` type when possible.
204	
205	You can manually run `basedpyright` using `uv`:
206	
207	```sh
208	uv run basedpyright
209	```
210	
211	Sometimes libraries may not include enough type information for `basedpyright`.
212	In these instances you can create type stubs. This process can be done by hand,
213	or you can try to use `basedpyright` to generate said stubs for you via a
214	command like:
215	
216	```sh
217	uv run basedpyright --createstub library
218	```
219	
220	These stubs would be placed in the `typings/` directory at the root of the
221	project.
222	
223	Sometimes type stubs may not be enough, in this case you can add [ignore
224	comments](https://docs.basedpyright.com/latest/configuration/comments/) to the
225	code.
226	
227	```py
228	# pyright: ignore
229	```
230	
231	You can also edit the `pyrightconfig.json` and add specific libraries to
232	`allowedUntypedLibraries` to allow a whole library to be untyped.
233	
234	#### boto3
235	
236	For `boto3` type stubs there is the package
237	[boto3-stubs](https://pypi.org/project/boto3-stubs/) which suggests generating
238	them yourself using a call similar to:
239	
240	```sh
241	uvx --with 'boto3==1.42.92' mypy-boto3-builder
242	```
243	
244	These have been stored in the `vendored/` directory.
245	
246	When you refer to these types, you should use `TYPE_CHECKING` to guard their
247	inclusion in your code and replace them with stub definitions. For example:
248	
249	```py
250	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
251	
252	if TYPE_CHECKING:
253	    from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient
254	    from types_boto3_dynamodb.type_defs import (
255	        AttributeValueTypeDef,
256	        UniversalAttributeValueTypeDef,
257	    )
258	else:
259	    DynamoDBClient = Any
260	    AttributeValueTypeDef = dict[str, Any]
261	    UniversalAttributeValueTypeDef = dict[str, Any]
262	```
263	
264	This is because the types are not included when the Python code is packaged.
265	
266	### black
267	
268	[black](https://black.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) is a code formatter for
269	Python.
270	
271	You can run `black` directly on a specific file using:
272	
273	```sh
274	uv run black path/to/file.py
275	```
276	
277	`black` is used to ensure all Python code follows the same standard of
278	formatting.
279

1	from typing import Any
2	
3	from aws_lambda_powertools import Logger
4	from aws_lambda_powertools.tracing import Tracer
5	from aws_lambda_powertools.utilities.typing import LambdaContext
6	from dotenv import load_dotenv
7	
8	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.erasure_request import ErasureRequest
9	
10	from .request_repository import RequestRepository, get_request_repository
11	
12	# Load .env file, mostly useful for testing
13	_ = load_dotenv()
14	
15	logger = Logger()
16	tracer = Tracer()  # Sets service via POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME env var
17	
18	request_repo: RequestRepository = get_request_repository()
19	
20	
21	def request_from_event(
22	    _event: dict[Any, Any],  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
23	) -> ErasureRequest:
24	    """Convert the input event into an ErasureRequest"""
25	    # TODO: Implement erasure request conversion
26	    raise NotImplementedError("Request conversion not implemented")
27	
28	
29	@tracer.capture_lambda_handler
30	@logger.inject_lambda_context
31	def lambda_handler(
32	    event: dict[Any, Any],  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
33	    _context: LambdaContext,
34	) -> None:
35	    """
36	    Entrypoint for AWS Lambda that does item creation.
37	
38	    This should be very simple, the Lambda should simply create an entry in the
39	    underlying storage, another lambda or piece of compute will process them.
40	    """
41	
42	    request: ErasureRequest = request_from_event(event)
43	    request_repo.create(request)
44

1	import os
2	from datetime import datetime, timedelta
3	from typing import Any, Literal
4	
5	from aws_lambda_powertools import Logger
6	from aws_lambda_powertools.tracing import Tracer
7	from aws_lambda_powertools.utilities.typing import LambdaContext
8	from dotenv import load_dotenv
9	
10	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_processor import RequestProcessor
11	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import (
12	    RequestRepository,
13	    get_request_repository,
14	)
15	
16	SAFETY_MARGIN_MS_ENV_VAR: Literal["PROCESS_SAFETY_MARGIN_MS"] = (
17	    "PROCESS_SAFETY_MARGIN_MS"
18	)
19	DEFAULT_SAFETY_MARGIN_MS: Literal[1000] = 1000
20	
21	# Load .env file, mostly useful for testing
22	_ = load_dotenv()
23	
24	logger = Logger()
25	tracer = Tracer()  # Sets service via POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME env var
26	
27	request_repo: RequestRepository = get_request_repository()
28	processor: RequestProcessor = RequestProcessor(request_repo)
29	
30	
31	def get_safety_margin_ms() -> int:
32	    """Get the safety margin in milliseconds from the environment"""
33	    from_env = os.getenv("PROCESS_SAFETY_MARGIN_MS")
34	    safety_margin_ms: int = DEFAULT_SAFETY_MARGIN_MS
35	    if from_env is not None:
36	        try:
37	            safety_margin_ms = int(from_env)
38	        except ValueError:
39	            logger.error(
40	                "Could not parse %s value %s as an integer",
41	                SAFETY_MARGIN_MS_ENV_VAR,
42	                from_env,
43	            )
44	    return safety_margin_ms
45	
46	
47	def get_timeout_datetime(remaining_millis: int) -> datetime:
48	    """Convert a remaining millis into a datetime"""
49	    delta = timedelta(milliseconds=remaining_millis)
50	    return datetime.now() + delta
51	
52	
53	@tracer.capture_lambda_handler
54	@logger.inject_lambda_context
55	def lambda_handler(
56	    _event: dict[Any, Any],  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
57	    context: LambdaContext,
58	) -> None:
59	    """
60	    Entrypoint for AWS Lambda that does erasure request processing.
61	    """
62	
63	    # Add a margin for safety so Lambdas don't timeout mid process
64	    safety_margin_ms: int = get_safety_margin_ms()
65	
66	    remaining_millis = context.get_remaining_time_in_millis() - safety_margin_ms
67	    timeout: datetime = get_timeout_datetime(remaining_millis)
68	    logger.info(
69	        f"Processing events with a timeout of {remaining_millis}ms until {timeout}"
70	    )
71	
72	    # Actual processing logic should be isolated in the processor class
73	    processor.process(timeout)
74

1	from dataclasses import dataclass, fields
2	from datetime import datetime
3	from typing import Literal, override
4	
5	type ErasureState = Literal[
6	    "new", "in-progress", "phase-1", "phase-2", "phase-3", "done", "need-more-detail"
7	]
8	"""Valid erasure states type"""
9	
10	type ErasureDetailKey = Literal[
11	    "first-name", "last-name", "email", "postcode", "phone-number", "rfl", "last-order"
12	]
13	"""Expected erasure details keys"""
14	
15	VALID_STATES: tuple[ErasureState, ...] = (
16	    "new",
17	    "in-progress",
18	    "phase-1",
19	    "phase-2",
20	    "phase-3",
21	    "done",
22	    "need-more-detail",
23	)
24	"""Valid erasure states"""
25	
26	
27	@dataclass
28	class ErasureRequest:
29	    """
30	    An Erasure request as submitted by a source system.
31	
32	    The normal source system is OneTrust.
33	    """
34	
35	    id: str
36	    """
37	    A unique identifier for this erasure request.
38	    This should come from the source system.
39	    """
40	
41	    source: str
42	    """The source of this Erasure request"""
43	
44	    state: ErasureState
45	    """The state that the request is in"""
46	
47	    date_issued: datetime
48	    """The date (and time) that this request was issued at"""
49	
50	    # TODO: Perhaps turn erasure_details into its own domain object
51	    erasure_details: dict[ErasureDetailKey, str]
52	    """
53	    Specific details for this erasure.
54	
55	    This is a sensitive field as it will contain the likes of email addresses,
56	    addresses etc.
57	
58	    The erasure details we normally get from OneTrust are:
59	
60	    - First Name
61	    - Last Name
62	    - Email
63	    - Postcode
64	
65	    With the optional ones being:
66	
67	    - Phone Number
68	    - RFL Number
69	    - Last Order Number
70	    """
71	
72	    # Below code attempts to omit the erasure_details from inclusing in strings
73	    # by mistake
74	
75	    def _safe_dict(self) -> dict[str, str]:
76	        return {
77	            f.name: getattr(self, f.name)
78	            for f in fields(self)
79	            if f.name not in ["erasure_details"]
80	        }
81	
82	    @override
83	    def __str__(self):
84	        d = self._safe_dict()
85	        return f"ErasureRequest(id={d['id']}, source={d['source']}, state={d['state']}, date_issued={d['date_issued']})"
86	
87	    @override
88	    def __repr__(self):
89	        d = self._safe_dict()
90	        return f"ErasureRequest(**{d})"
91	
92	    @property
93	    def date_issued_ms(self) -> int:
94	        """The date and time issued as a unix timestamp (milliseconds)"""
95	        timestamp_ms = int(self.date_issued.timestamp() * 1000)
96	        return timestamp_ms
97

content.text:
I have enough information. Acknowledging plan mode - no edits made. Here are the findings.

## Exploration Report: dns-de-rte-orchestrator SAM/Lambda setup

### 1. template.yaml (`/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/template.yaml`)

**Parameters** (lines 8-17): `Environment`, `DynamoDBTable`, `DynamoDBTableGSI`.

**Resources:**

- **`ServerlessAccessManager`** (lines 20-31): `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager` resource. Invokes a shared PaaS Lambda (`paas-serverless-access-manager` in `eu-west-1`). Supplies `FunctionName`, `VpcConfig`, and `LambdaExecutionRoleArn` outputs to consumers. Configured with `VpcExecution: "true"`, `DatadogLogging: "true"`, and a `Targets.DynamoDBTable` entry granting DynamoDB access.

- **`CreateFunction`** (lines 33-54): Full definition:
  - `Type: AWS::Serverless::Function`, `BuildMethod: makefile`
  - `FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName` (name `data-rte-orchestrator-create-request-${Environment}`)
  - `Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.creator_lambda.lambda_handler`
  - `Runtime: python3.13`, `MemorySize: 256`, `Timeout: 30`, `ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 10`, `Tracing: Active`
  - `VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig`
  - `Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn`
  - Env vars: `REPOSITORY_TYPE=dynamodb`, `DYNAMO_DB_TABLE`, `DYNAMO_DB_INDEX`, `POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME`

No `Events` (no API Gateway triggers), no `Outputs`, no explicit `AWS::ApiGateway::*` or `AWS::Serverless::Api`. Processor Lambda is not yet in the template (TODO.md line 58).

### 2. samconfig.toml (`/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/samconfig.toml`)

Two deploy envs (`dev`, `prod`), both in `eu-west-1`, account `334512769709`, deploy role `arn:aws:iam::334512769709:role/CFDeploy`, S3 bucket `hbi-dns-infra-datalake`. Capabilities `CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND`. Stacks: `data-rte-orchestrator-dev/prod`. Parameter overrides set Environment + DynamoDB table/GSI names per env.

### 3. creator_lambda.py (`/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py`)

**Not API-Gateway-ready.** Handler signature (line 31): `lambda_handler(event: dict[Any, Any], _context) -> None`. Uses generic dict event, decorated with `@tracer.capture_lambda_handler` and `@logger.inject_lambda_context`. There is no `APIGatewayProxyEvent` typing, no `event_source` decorator, no response object — return type is `None`. The conversion function `request_from_event` (lines 21-26) is a stub that raises `NotImplementedError("Request conversion not implemented")`. Will need adaptation for API Gateway: parse body/headers, produce a proxy-integration response `{statusCode, body, headers}`, add error handling for HTTP status codes.

### 4. Makefile (`/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/Makefile`)

`build-CreateFunction` target: exports non-dev deps via `uv export` to `.aws-sam-requirements.txt`, installs to `${ARTIFACTS_DIR}`, copies `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/` into artifacts, cleans up. Invoked by SAM per the `BuildMethod: makefile` metadata. Would need a matching `build-ProcessFunction` target if a second Lambda is added.

### 5. Existing API Gateway / HTTP code

**None.** `grep` matched `http`/`rest` only in unrelated areas (`customer_api_client.py` makes outbound HTTP calls, `request_processor.py`/`request_repository.py`/`erasure_request.py` use unrelated substrings). No `AWS::Serverless::Api`, no `APIGatewayProxyEvent`, no `event_source`, no Powertools `event_handler` (e.g. `APIGatewayRestResolver`). `aws_lambda_powertools` is already a dep (used for Logger/Tracer) — the `event_handler` module would be available without new deps.

### 6. README context (`/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/README.md`)

Project replaces a Tines-based GDPR Right-to-Erasure orchestrator. Two Lambdas intended: `creator_lambda` (creates new request in DynamoDB) and `processor_lambda` (processes queued items, triggered separately — likely scheduled). README does not specify invocation source for the creator; OneTrust is named (erasure_request.py line 32) as the normal source system. Deploy via `sam build` + `sam deploy --config-file samconfig.toml --config-env {dev,prod}`. Note in README lines 124-128: `sam local invoke` requires commenting out `ServerlessAccessManager` because the custom resource cannot run locally.

### Key Patterns / Takeaways for adding API Gateway

- **IAM pattern:** Role/VPC/FunctionName all come from the `ServerlessAccessManager` custom resource — you cannot define a standard `Role:` or `Policies:` inline. Any new permissions must go through `Targets` on the custom resource (see DynamoDB target example at template.yaml lines 28-31). API Gateway typically doesn't require Lambda-side IAM (API GW invokes via resource policy), so the existing role pattern is likely fine, but the `Events:` property on the function may be the simplest integration path rather than standalone `AWS::Serverless::Api`.
- **Handler adaptation required:** `request_from_event` is unimplemented and the handler returns `None`. Needs to parse API Gateway proxy body JSON into `ErasureRequest` and return a proxy response dict. Consider Powertools `APIGatewayRestResolver` (already an installed dep).
- **Two-Lambda intent:** Processor Lambda still to be added (TODO line 58) — keep API Gateway config orthogonal.

### Critical file paths + line references

- `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/template.yaml:20-31` — ServerlessAccessManager
- `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/template.yaml:33-54` — CreateFunction
- `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py:21-26` — stub `request_from_event`
- `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py:29-43` — `lambda_handler`
- `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/erasure_request.py:27-70` — `ErasureRequest` dataclass (fields API Gateway body must supply)
- `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/Makefile:10-14` — build target
- `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/samconfig.toml:9-55` — dev/prod deploy config
- `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/TODO.md:56-65` — SAM deployment TODO state
